findings.title: Missing HTML character escaping in JSON output
findings.rationale: The diff removes code that escaped '<', '>', and '&' in JSON output (untrusted input source: string s passed to AppendJSONString). This prevents XSS if JSON is embedded in HTML without additional escaping. The standard json.Encoder escapes these by default (SetEscapeHTML=true), but the custom function now never escapes them, deviating from expected behavior.
findings.suggestion: Reintroduce HTML character escaping for '<', '>', '&' when mimicking standard JSON encoder behavior

evidence.ref: Go standard library json.Encoder.SetEscapeHTML defaults to true
OWASP XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet: Escape HTML special characters in context
